channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Speeding vechiles, 30th May
item.description:
​Subject: Proposal for Traffic Calming Measures and Speed Limit Reduction on North Cray Road ​I am writing to formally request a review of the current traffic management and speed limits on North Cray Road. Specifically, I propose the following amendments to improve road safety and environmental quality: ​Implementation of an Average Speed Check System (40 mph): Replacing static enforcement with an average speed camera system to ensure consistent speed compliance across the length of the road. ​Speed Limit Reduction to 30 mph at Existing Camera Locations: Lowering the local limit near current static camera sites to protect key pedestrian zones. ​This proposal is supported by several critical safety, environmental, and community factors outlined below: ​Frequent Speed Violations: Vehicles are routinely observed traveling in excess of 60 mph on this stretch of road, indicating that current static enforcement measures are insufficient to deter speeding. ​Noise and Environmental Pollution: The existing static cameras cause an "acceleration-deceleration" pattern. Drivers brake abruptly for the camera and accelerate aggressively immediately after passing it. This constant acceleration significantly increases both engine noise pollution and localized exhaust emissions. ​Pedestrian Vulnerability: There is an existing pedestrian crossing on this route. Maintaining a 40 mph limit through a designated crossing zone introduces unnecessary risk, especially given the lack of compliance. ​Lack of Safe Crossings: Given the overall length of North Cray Road, there are currently only two safe designated crossing locations. This scarcity makes crossing highly hazardous for pedestrians when vehicles are consistently traveling at excessive speeds. ​Shared Road Use: This route is actively used by horse riders. High-speed traffic and sudden engine acceleration pose a severe risk of startling horses, endangering both the riders and motorists. ​Impact on Local Wildlife: The high speeds on this road result in a severe toll on local wildlife, with animal casualties occurring on a weekly basis. ​Implementing an average speed check system will smooth out traffic flow, eliminate aggressive acceleration cycles, and create a significantly safer environment for residents, pedestrians, and vulnerable road users alike. ​Thank you for your time and consideration of this urgent matter. I look forward to your response regarding any planned traffic assessments for North Cray Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): North Cray Road, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping / dumped fridge, 30th May
item.description:
Fly-tipping issue involving a dumped fridge

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Birchdene Drive, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old mattress and rubbish, 30th May
item.description:
This pathway is becoming unsafe and unpleasant to use. A mattress has been dumped there, and the route towards the bus stop: Thamesmead / Grasshaven Way (Stop X) and towards Morrison’s is full of rubbish, including old coats, clothing, boxes and a broken umbrella. There are no bins in this area, and an overgrown tree is now starting to obstruct the path to Thamesmead / Grasshaven Way bus stop (Stop X).

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Birchdene Drive, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vechile, 30th May
item.description:
This vechile has been here for over 6months with a tow bar and flat tyres and has not moved i have knocked on neighbours doors and no- one has claimed the vechile we are struggling with parking. Im have raised this before however nothing was done can you please contact the owner to move It is also parked in a way that restricts parking space.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Honeyden Road, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: LIttering and Fly tipping, 30th May
item.description:
Under the trees along Cambridge Road side of roundabout there is significant rubbish this evening including a pile of boulders/rocks. The rest of the roundabout looks great and well kept. I regularly walk this area and significant increase in litter noted today.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cambridge Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of litter and fly tipping down the footpath, 30th May
item.description:
As you walk down the foot path. Lots of litter and fly tipping. Needs to be cleaned up and a sign put up about fly tipping.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 36, Imperial Way, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Close to junction with St James Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Webster Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Halfway between the zebra crossing and St Pancras international bus stop, 30th May
item.description:
On Thursday 28 May, my father and sister with her two kids got off the 214 bus at st Pancras international and were walking towards the zebra crossing. My sister tripped on an uneven pavement and before she could tell my father to be careful, he had already fell flat on his face on the floor. Ripped jeans and bleeding on his knee and majority of impact on his nose and mouth which were bleeding. This is a route that he takes every Tuesday and Friday and the last time this route was taken was Tuesday 26 May where there was no issues. He was assisted up by two construction men working nearby and walked into St Pancras International station. He was initially refused medical assistance at the station because staff advised the accident took place external from the station. Only until they saw the impact on his face, they sought assistance.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Midland Road, Somers Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars, 30th May
item.description:
Cars parking on yellow lines over night every night either start putting tickets on or get rid of yellow line sick of parking half way up the road cos you wanna put yellow lines but don't control it joke of a council sort it out

item.title: Street lighting is not working at the bottom section of Ruskin Road. The lamps in this area are completely out, leaving the road dark at night and creating a safety concern for pedestrians and vehicles. The top of the road is unaffected., 30th May
item.description:
No working streetlights at the bottom of Ruskin Road. The area becomes completely dark at night, creating safety concerns for pedestrians and making it uncomfortable to walk through after dark.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ruskin Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light pollution.. Street Light needs a cap/shield!, 30th May
item.description:
Please can this be reported as light pollution, with a view to something being added to this street light so it doesn’t shine into my property. The bright white LED is reflecting directly into two bed rooms making sleep difficult! Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Somerhill Avenue, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cycle lane, 30th May
item.description:
Dangerous road defect in cycle lane which causes cyclists to go over handle bars still not fixed after months of reports.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich

item.title: There’s no lights at all when u come down the road and my flat 41-61 William court got no lights inside at all and soon as it gets dark it’s scary, 30th May
item.description:
There’s no lights at all when u come down the road and my flat 41-61 William court got no lights inside at all and soon as it gets dark it’s scary and we would really appreciate if you’d help us fix these lights here and put some bench at the bushes as well as there’s loads of us that be chilling every single day here and enjoying the view all we need is some lights and one bench 😂

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Clive Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cctv, 30th May
item.description:
Camera with audio system on a public footpath repeating audio “You are in a red area, please leave” to every person it detects.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
